phrases.intent_en: Ask for 500 grams of Périgord foie gras
phrases.text_local: Je voudrais cinq cents grammes de foie gras du Périgord, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DRAY sank-SAHN GRAM duh FWAH-grah doo pay-ree-GOR seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Marché des Capucins is Bordeaux's historic covered market, nicknamed 'le ventre de Bordeaux' (the belly of Bordeaux).

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the oysters are from Arcachon Bay
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que vos huîtres viennent du Bassin d'Arcachon ?
phrases.pronunciation: es-kuh voh ZWEE-truh VYEN doo bah-SAN dar-kah-SHON
phrases.cultural_note: Arcachon oysters are a local speciality and are sold freshly shucked at the market with bread and white wine.

responses.english: Yes, from Arcachon, arrived this morning.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to taste a slice of cheese before buying
phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous me faire goûter ce fromage avant que je l'achète ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-RYAY-voo muh FAIR goo-TAY suh froh-MAZH ah-VAHN kuh zhuh lah-SHET
phrases.cultural_note: Cheese vendors at French markets will typically offer a small taste without hesitation.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the market opens in the morning
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure ouvre le marché le matin ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel ur OO-vruh luh mar-SHAY luh mah-TAN
phrases.cultural_note: The Marché des Capucins operates Tuesday through Sunday from around 6 am; Sunday morning is the busiest and most festive.

phrases.intent_en: Ask the price per kilo of strawberries
phrases.text_local: Quel est le prix au kilo pour ces fraises ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel eh luh PREE oh kee-LOH poor say FRAIZ
phrases.cultural_note: Local Périgord strawberries are in season from May to July and are considered among France's finest.
